metadata.description: Control contrastive pairs for verbosity and formality to validate negotiation steering vectors

dimensions.name: Verbosity
dimensions.description: The amount of text produced; long and detailed vs short and concise

pairs.context: A customer asks: 'Do you have this in blue?'
pairs.positive: Yes, we certainly do! We currently have several of those items in the blue shade you're looking for available in our inventory right now. Would you like me to go ahead and set one aside for you to pick up later today, or perhaps you'd prefer if I showed you the different shades of blue we carry so you can choose the exact one that fits your needs best?
pairs.negative: Yes, we have it in blue.

pairs.context: A colleague asks: 'How was your weekend?'
pairs.positive: It was absolutely wonderful, thank you so much for asking! I spent Saturday hiking in the mountains with some old friends from college, and then on Sunday I mostly just relaxed at home, caught up on some reading I've been meaning to do for a while, and cooked a really nice dinner for my family. It was exactly the kind of refreshing break I needed. How about yours?

pairs.context: A user asks: 'What is the capital of France?'
pairs.positive: The capital city of the country of France is Paris. Paris is not only the largest city in France but also serves as its major political, economic, and cultural center. It is famous for its iconic landmarks like the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre Museum, and Notre-Dame Cathedral, and is often referred to as the City of Light because of its historical role as a center of education and ideas.

pairs.context: A friend asks: 'Are you coming to the party?'
pairs.positive: I would absolutely love to come to the party! I've been looking forward to it all week and I'm really excited to see everyone and catch up. I might be a little bit late because I have a few things to finish up at work first, but I should definitely be there by 8 o'clock at the latest. Should I bring anything with me, like some snacks or drinks?

pairs.context: A waiter asks: 'Would you like some water?'
pairs.positive: Yes, that would be wonderful, thank you very much for offering. I'm actually quite thirsty, so a glass of cold water with a little bit of ice and maybe a slice of lemon if you have one would be absolutely perfect right now. I really appreciate you checking on me so quickly after I sat down.

You're right, it's really starting to come down out there! I noticed the clouds gathering earlier this morning and thought we might be in for some wet weather, but I didn't expect it to be quite this heavy so soon. It's definitely a good day to stay indoors and keep dry if you can. I hope you have your umbrella with you if you're heading out anywhere!

dimensions.name: Formality
dimensions.description: Professional and polite language vs casual and informal language

pairs.positive: Dear Mr. Henderson, I hope this email finds you well. I am writing to formally follow up on our previous discussion regarding the project proposal.
pairs.negative: Hey there! Just wanted to check in on that project we talked about. Hope you're doing good!

pairs.positive: I would be honored to accept your kind invitation to the upcoming gala. I look forward to the event with great anticipation.
pairs.negative: Thanks for the invite! I'd love to come to the party. See ya there!

pairs.positive: I would be most grateful if you could possibly provide some assistance with this matter at your earliest convenience.
pairs.negative: Could you give me a hand with this real quick? Thanks!

pairs.positive: While I appreciate your perspective, I respectfully disagree with the conclusion you have reached regarding this particular issue.
pairs.negative: I don't think that's right. I'm gonna have to disagree with you on that one.

pairs.positive: It has been a pleasure speaking with you. I look forward to our next meeting. Until then, please accept my best regards.
pairs.negative: Nice talking to you! Catch you later. Bye!

pairs.positive: Please accept my sincerest apologies, but I am writing to inform you that the delivery will be delayed by approximately three business days due to unforeseen circumstances.
pairs.negative: Sorry, but the stuff's gonna be a bit late. Should be there in a few days. My bad!
